name: Data Security and Protection Toolkit (DSPT) Ontology
description: Ontology representing the NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit (DSPT), the UK health sector's mandatory self-assessment framework aligned to the 10 National Data Guardian (NDG) Data Security Standards. Models 10 standards, 44 assertions, 149 evidence items across 4 organisation categories (NHS Trusts, CCG/ALBs, Others, GP), with mandatory/optional classification. The DSPT is the primary compliance mechanism for NHS organisations and health/social care providers, bridging to NCSC CAF for cyber resilience and GDPR for data protection.
oaa:domain: UK Health Sector Data Security & Protection

oaa:sourceFramework.name: Data Security and Protection Toolkit
oaa:sourceFramework.alignedTo: National Data Guardian 10 Data Security Standards
applicability: Category 3: Other health and social care organisations

oaa:imports.name: GRC-FW-ONT
oaa:imports.description: RCSG Framework providing ComplianceFramework for DSPT status tracking

oaa:imports.name: NCSC-CAF-ONT
oaa:imports.description: NCSC CAF providing ContributingOutcome for cross-framework CAF-DSPT mapping

oaa:imports.name: GDPR-ONT
oaa:imports.description: GDPR providing DataProtectionPrinciple and DataSubjectRight for DSPT Standards 1-2 alignment

oaa:joinPatterns.name: DSPT to CAF Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: DSPT standards map to NCSC CAF Contributing Outcomes for dual-framework compliance
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: NHS organisations meeting both DSPT and CAF requirements with shared evidence

oaa:joinPatterns.name: DSPT to GDPR Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: DSPT Standards 1-2 map to GDPR principles and data subject rights
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Demonstrate GDPR compliance through DSPT evidence

oaa:joinPatterns.name: DSPT Assessment to Standards Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: DSPT assessments evaluate all 10 NDG standards and fulfil GDPR compliance obligations when Standards Met
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Complete assessment traceability from submission through standards to CAF principle alignment

entities.name: DSPTStandard
entities.description: One of the 10 National Data Guardian Data Security Standards assessed by the DSPT. Standards are grouped into three themes: People (1-3), Process (4-7), and Technology (8-10).

oaa:properties.name: standardNumber
oaa:properties.description: Standard number (1-10)

oaa:properties.name: standardName
oaa:properties.description: Full name of the NDG Data Security Standard

oaa:properties.name: theme
oaa:properties.description: Theme grouping: People, Process, or Technology

oaa:properties.name: assertionCount
oaa:properties.description: Number of assertions within this standard

oaa:properties.name: evidenceItemCount
oaa:properties.description: Total number of evidence items across all assertions

oaa:properties.name: mandatoryEvidenceCount
oaa:properties.description: Number of mandatory evidence items for Category 1 (NHS Trusts)

oaa:instances.oaa:ndgStandard: All staff ensure that personal confidential data is handled, stored and transmitted securely, whether in electronic or paper form
All staff understand their responsibilities under the National Data Guardian's Data Security Standards
All staff complete appropriate annual data security training and pass a mandatory test
Personal confidential data is only accessible to staff who need it for their current role and access is removed when no longer required
Processes are reviewed at least annually to identify and improve processes which have caused breaches or near misses
Cyber attacks against services are identified and resisted and CareCERT security advice is responded to
A continuity plan is in place to respond to threats to data security, including significant data breaches or near misses, and is regularly tested
All unsupported software and hardware is identified, documented, and risk-assessed, with security risks managed
A strategy is in place for protecting IT systems from cyber threats which is based on a proven cyber security framework such as Cyber Essentials
IT suppliers are held accountable via contracts for protecting the personal confidential data they process and meeting the NDG Standards

entities.name: DSPTAssertion
entities.description: A high-level assertion within a DSPT Standard that an organisation must demonstrate through evidence items. Each assertion groups related evidence requirements under a common security or data protection theme.

oaa:properties.name: assertionRef
oaa:properties.description: Assertion reference number (e.g., 1.1, 4.2, 9.5)

oaa:properties.name: assertionText
oaa:properties.description: Full text of the assertion statement

oaa:properties.name: evidenceItemCount
oaa:properties.description: Number of evidence items under this assertion

oaa:instances.assertionText: The organisation has a framework in place to support Lawfulness, Fairness and Transparency
Individuals' rights are respected and supported
Accountability and Governance in place for data protection and data security
Records are maintained appropriately
Staff are supported in understanding their obligations under the NDG Data Security Standards
There has been an assessment of data security and protection training needs across the organisation
Staff pass the data security and protection mandatory test
Staff with specialist roles receive data security and protection training suitable to their role
Leaders and board members receive suitable data protection and security training
The organisation maintains a current record of staff and their roles
The organisation assures good management and maintenance of identity and access control for its networks and information systems
All staff understand that their activities on IT systems will be monitored and recorded for security purposes
You closely manage privileged user access to networks and information systems supporting the essential service
You ensure your passwords are suitable for the information you are protecting
Process reviews are held at least once per year where data security is put at risk and following data security incidents
Participation in reviews is comprehensive, and clinicians are actively involved
Action is taken to address problem processes as a result of feedback at meetings or in year
A confidential system for reporting data security and protection breaches and near misses is in place and actively used
All user devices are subject to anti-virus protections while email services benefit from spam filtering and protection
Known vulnerabilities are acted on based on advice from NHS Digital, and lessons are learned from previous incidents
Organisations have a defined, planned and communicated response to data security incidents
There is an effective test of the continuity plan and disaster recovery plan for data security incidents
You have the capability to enact your incident response plan, including effective limitation of impact
All software and hardware has been surveyed to understand if it is supported and up to date
Unsupported software and hardware is categorised and documented, and data security risks are managed
Supported systems are kept up-to-date with the latest security patches
You manage known vulnerabilities in your network and information systems to prevent disruption
All networking components have had their default passwords changed
A penetration test has been scoped and undertaken
Systems which handle sensitive information or key services shall be protected from exploitation of known vulnerabilities
You have demonstrable confidence in the effectiveness of the security of your technology, people, and processes
You securely configure the network and information systems that support the delivery of essential services
The organisation is protected by a well managed firewall
The organisation can name its suppliers, the products and services they deliver and the contract durations
Basic due diligence has been undertaken against each supplier that handles personal information
All disputes between the organisation and its suppliers have been recorded with data security risks documented
All instances where organisations cannot comply with NDG Standards because of supplier issues are recorded and discussed at board
The organisation understands and manages security risks to networks and information systems from the supply chain

entities.name: EvidenceItem
entities.description: An individual evidence requirement within a DSPT Assertion. Evidence items vary by organisation category and may be mandatory or optional. Input types include Text, Document, Date, URL, Yes/No, and Number.

oaa:properties.name: evidenceRef
oaa:properties.description: Evidence reference number (e.g., 1.1.1, 4.2.3, 9.5.4)

oaa:properties.name: evidenceText
oaa:properties.description: The evidence requirement text as presented to the organisation

oaa:properties.name: inputType
oaa:properties.description: Type of input expected (Text, Document, Date, URL, Yes/No, Number)

oaa:properties.name: mandatoryCategory1
oaa:properties.description: Whether this evidence is mandatory for NHS Trusts and CSUs

oaa:properties.name: mandatoryCategory2
oaa:properties.description: Whether this evidence is mandatory for CCGs and ALBs

oaa:properties.name: mandatoryCategory3
oaa:properties.description: Whether this evidence is mandatory for other health/social care organisations

oaa:properties.name: mandatoryCategory4
oaa:properties.description: Whether this evidence is mandatory for GP practices

oaa:properties.name: exemptCyberEssentialsPlus
oaa:properties.description: Whether organisations with Cyber Essentials Plus are exempt from this item

oaa:properties.name: exemptISO27001
oaa:properties.description: Whether organisations with ISO 27001 are exempt from this item

oaa:properties.name: approachingStandardsCategory
oaa:properties.description: Category for 'Approaching Standards' status if applicable

entities.name: DSPTAssessment
entities.description: An annual DSPT submission by an organisation, recording completion status for all mandatory evidence items and the overall assessment outcome (Standards Met, Approaching Standards, Standards Not Met).

oaa:properties.name: assessmentId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this DSPT submission

oaa:properties.name: organisationName
oaa:properties.description: Name of the submitting organisation

oaa:properties.name: odsCode
oaa:properties.description: Organisation Data Service (ODS) code identifying the NHS organisation

oaa:properties.name: organisationCategory
oaa:properties.description: DSPT organisation category (1-4)

oaa:properties.name: submissionDate
oaa:properties.description: Date of DSPT submission (annual deadline typically 30 June)

oaa:properties.name: assessmentYear
oaa:properties.description: Assessment year (e.g., 2024-25)

oaa:properties.name: overallStatus
oaa:properties.description: Overall DSPT assessment outcome

oaa:properties.name: mandatoryItemsMet
oaa:properties.description: Number of mandatory evidence items satisfied

oaa:properties.name: mandatoryItemsTotal
oaa:properties.description: Total mandatory evidence items for this organisation category

oaa:properties.name: optionalItemsCompleted
oaa:properties.description: Number of optional evidence items completed

oaa:properties.name: improvementPlanRequired
oaa:properties.description: Whether an improvement plan is required for non-compliant items

entities.name: CAFDSPTMapping
entities.description: Cross-framework mapping between DSPT Standards/Assertions and NCSC CAF Contributing Outcomes, enabling NHS organisations to demonstrate dual compliance with shared evidence artefacts.

oaa:properties.name: dsptStandardNumber
oaa:properties.description: DSPT Standard number being mapped

oaa:properties.name: cafOutcomeCode
oaa:properties.description: NCSC CAF Contributing Outcome code being mapped to

oaa:properties.name: mappingStrength
oaa:properties.description: Strength of the mapping: Direct, Partial, or Related

oaa:properties.name: sharedEvidence
oaa:properties.description: Description of evidence artefacts that satisfy both frameworks

oaa:instances.sharedEvidence: Board-level data protection governance and accountability
Information asset registers, data flow mapping, ROPA
Mandatory security awareness training completion records
Specialist role-based security training and board-level training
Privileged user access management and monitoring
IdAM policies, JML processes, access reviews
Post-incident process reviews and lessons learned
Vulnerability management and patching from CareCERT advisories
Incident response plans and communication procedures
Response and recovery capability, BCP/DR plans
Continuity and disaster recovery plan testing
Hardware and software asset inventories and lifecycle management
Vulnerability management for unsupported systems and patching
Secure by design principles and pen testing
Secure configuration, default password changes, firewall management
Supplier inventory, due diligence, contract management, supply chain risk

entities.name: ComplianceFramework
entities.description: External GRC-FW entity for DSPT certification status tracking.

entities.name: ContributingOutcome
entities.description: External NCSC-CAF entity for cross-framework mapping.

entities.name: CAFPrinciple
entities.description: External NCSC-CAF entity for principle-level mapping.

entities.name: DataProtectionPrinciple
entities.description: External GDPR entity for data protection principle alignment.

entities.name: DataSubjectRight
entities.description: External GDPR entity for data subject rights alignment.

entities.name: ComplianceObligation
entities.description: External GDPR entity for compliance obligation alignment.

oaa:enumerations.name: DSPT Theme
oaa:enumerations.description: The three themes grouping the 10 NDG Data Security Standards

values.name: People
values.description: Standards 1-3: Personal data handling, staff responsibilities, and training

values.name: Process
values.description: Standards 4-7: Data access management, process reviews, incident response, and continuity planning

values.name: Technology
values.description: Standards 8-10: Unsupported systems, IT protection, and accountable suppliers

oaa:enumerations.name: DSPT Status
oaa:enumerations.description: Possible outcomes of a DSPT annual assessment

values.name: Standards Exceeded
values.description: All mandatory items met plus significant optional items completed

values.name: Standards Met
values.description: All mandatory evidence items for the organisation's category have been satisfied

values.name: Approaching Standards
values.description: Organisation is making progress but has not yet met all mandatory items — improvement plan required

values.name: Standards Not Met
values.description: Significant mandatory items remain unsatisfied — action plan required

values.name: Not Published
values.description: Assessment has not been submitted by the annual deadline

oaa:enumerations.name: Organisation Category
oaa:enumerations.description: DSPT organisation categories determining which evidence items apply

values.name: Category 1
values.description: NHS Trusts and Commissioning Support Units (CSUs) — fullest set of mandatory requirements

values.name: Category 3
values.description: Other health and social care organisations (care homes, pharmacies, opticians, dentists)

oaa:enumerations.name: Input Type
oaa:enumerations.description: Types of evidence input accepted by the DSPT

values.name: Document
values.description: Uploaded document or link to evidence

values.name: URL
values.description: Web link to evidence or system

relationships.name: hasAssertion
relationships.description: A DSPTStandard contains one or more DSPTAssertions

relationships.name: hasEvidenceItem
relationships.description: A DSPTAssertion requires one or more EvidenceItems to demonstrate compliance

relationships.name: hasCAFMapping
relationships.description: A DSPTStandard has mappings to NCSC CAF Contributing Outcomes for cross-framework compliance

relationships.name: mapsToCAFOutcome
relationships.description: A CAFDSPTMapping links a DSPT standard to a specific NCSC CAF Contributing Outcome

relationships.name: implementsGDPRPrinciple
relationships.description: DSPT Standards 1-2 assertions implement GDPR DataProtectionPrinciples

relationships.name: supportsDataSubjectRight
relationships.description: DSPT Standard 1.2 assertions support GDPR DataSubjectRights

relationships.name: registeredAsFramework
relationships.description: A DSPTAssessment registers as an GRC-FW ComplianceFramework for organisational compliance tracking

relationships.name: assessesStandard
relationships.description: A DSPTAssessment evaluates one or more DSPTStandards, recording evidence completion for each standard's mandatory and optional items

relationships.name: alignsToPrinciple
relationships.description: A DSPTStandard aligns to one or more NCSC CAF Principles at the principle level, complementing the finer-grained Contributing Outcome mappings via CAFDSPTMapping

relationships.name: fulfillsObligation
relationships.description: A DSPTAssessment with Standards Met status fulfills specific GDPR ComplianceObligations, particularly around data security (Article 32) and accountability (Article 5(2))

businessRules.name: Standards Met Requires All Mandatory Items
businessRules.action: THEN mandatoryItemsMet MUST equal mandatoryItemsTotal for the relevant OrganisationCategory

businessRules.name: Annual Submission Required
businessRules.condition: IF organisation is an NHS-funded body or health/social care provider
businessRules.action: THEN DSPTAssessment MUST be submitted annually by the deadline (typically 30 June)

businessRules.name: Approaching Standards Requires Improvement Plan
businessRules.action: THEN improvementPlanRequired MUST be true AND an improvement plan with target dates must be documented

businessRules.name: Cyber Essentials Plus Exemptions Apply
businessRules.condition: IF organisation holds current Cyber Essentials Plus certification
businessRules.action: THEN EvidenceItems with exemptCyberEssentialsPlus=true may be marked as met without additional evidence

businessRules.name: Category Determines Mandatory Scope
businessRules.action: THEN only EvidenceItems mandatory for that category are required for Standards Met status

businessRules.name: DSPT Standards Met Enables NHS Data Sharing
businessRules.condition: IF organisation needs to access NHS patient data or connect to HSCN
businessRules.action: THEN DSPTAssessment.overallStatus MUST be 'Standards Met' or 'Standards Exceeded'

crossOntologyReferences.description: DSPT-ONT bridges NHS health sector data security requirements to the wider RCSG compliance ecosystem. DSPT Standards map to NCSC CAF Contributing Outcomes for organisations subject to both frameworks (e.g., NHS Trusts designated as OES under NIS Regulations). Standards 1-2 align to GDPR data protection principles and data subject rights. DSPT assessment status registers as an GRC-FW ComplianceFramework entry for organisational compliance landscape tracking.

lineageAlignment.ncsc-caf:ContributingOutcome: NCSC CAF outcomes provide the cyber security framework that DSPT technology standards (8-10) align to
lineageAlignment.ncsc-caf:CAFPrinciple: NCSC CAF principles provide principle-level alignment for DSPT standards (e.g., Standard 9 aligns to CAF B4 System Security)
lineageAlignment.gdpr:DataProtectionPrinciple: GDPR principles underpin DSPT Standards 1-2 (data protection, lawfulness, rights)
lineageAlignment.gdpr:DataSubjectRight: GDPR data subject rights are implemented through DSPT Standard 1.2 assertions
lineageAlignment.gdpr:ComplianceObligation: GDPR compliance obligations (Article 32 security, Article 5(2) accountability) are fulfilled by DSPT Standards Met status
lineageAlignment.grc-fw:ComplianceFramework: GRC-FW tracks DSPT assessment status within organisational compliance posture
